questions.question: National Council for Vocational Training was set up in the year _______?
questions.explanation: The National Council for Vocational Training, an advisory body, was set up by the Government of India in 1956. The Council has been entrusted with the responsibilities of prescribing standards and curricula for craftsmen training, advising the Government of India on the overall policy and programmes, conducting All India Trade Tests and awarding National Trade Certificates.

questions.question: AICTE is headquartered in __________?
questions.explanation: All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE) was set up in November 1945 as a national-level Apex Advisory Body to conduct a survey on the facilities available for technical education and to promote development in the country in a coordinated and integrated manner. It is headquartered in New Delhi.

questions.question: Bar council of India was established in ______?
questions.explanation: The Bar Council of India was established in 1961 by Parliament under the Advocates Act, 1961. It sets standards for legal education and grants recognition to Universities whose degree in law will serve as qualification for enrolment as an advocate.

questions.question: Dental Council of India is financed by _________?
options: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
Ministry of Women and Child Development
questions.explanation: Dental Council of India is a Statutory Body incorporated under The Dentists Act, 1948 to regulate the Dental Education and the profession of Dentistry throughout India and it is financed by the Govt. of India in the Ministry of Health & Family Welfare.

questions.question: Institute for Plasma Research is headquartered in which state?
questions.explanation: Institute for Plasma Research is an autonomous R & D organization under the authority of Department of Atomic Energy (DAE), Government of India situated in Gujarat, India.

questions.question: Indian Statistical Institute is headquartered in ________?
questions.explanation: Indian Statistical Institute (ISI), a unique institution devoted to the research, teaching and application of statistics, natural sciences and social sciences. Founded by Professor P.C. Mahalanobis in Kolkata on 17th December, 1931.

questions.question: NAAC headquartered in Karnataka is related to which among the following fields?
questions.explanation: The NATIONAL ASSESSMENT AND ACCREDITATION COUNCIL (NAAC) is an autonomous body established by the University Grants Commission (UGC) of India to assess and accredit institutions of higher education in the country.

questions.question: National Council of Educational Research and Training was set up in the year ________?
questions.explanation: The National Council of Educational Research and Training (NCERT) is an autonomous organisation set up in 1961 by the Government of India to assist and advise the Central and State Governments on policies and programmes for qualitative improvement in school education.

questions.question: Rehabilitation Council of India(RCI) was set up in the year ______?
questions.explanation: Rehabilitation Council of India(RCI) was set up as a registered society in 1986.On September,1992 the RCI Act was enacted by Parliament and it became a Statutory Body on 22 June 1993. The mandate given to RCI is to regulate and monitor services given to persons with disability to standardise syllabi and to maintain a Central Rehabilitation Register of all qualified professionals and personnel working in the field of Rehabilitation and Special Education.

questions.question: Veterinary Council of India is headquartered in ________?
questions.explanation: Veterinary Council of India (VCI) is a statutory body which regulates veterinary practice in India. Established in 1984 it is based in New Delhi.

questions.question: What does "S" stand for in UPSC?
questions.explanation: UPSC stands for Union Public Service Commission. It is India's central recruiting agency. It is responsible for appointments to and examinations for All India services and central services.

questions.question: Central Institute of Educational Technology(CIET) is a constituent unit of _______?
questions.explanation: Central Institute of Educational Technology(CIET), a constituent unit of NCERT, came into existence in the year 1984 with the merger of Center for Educational Technology and Department of Teaching Aids. CIET is a premiere national institute of educational technology.

questions.question: All India Institute of Medical Sciences was established in _______?
questions.explanation: The AIIMS was created in 1956, as an autonomous institution through an Act of Parliament, to serve as a nucleus for nurturing excellence in all aspect of health care.

questions.question: International Volunteer Day is observed on _________?
questions.explanation: The International Volunteer Day (IVD) mandated by the UN General Assembly, is held each year on 5 December. It is viewed as a unique chance for volunteers and organizations to celebrate their efforts, to share their values, and to promote their work among their communities, non-governmental organizations (NGOs), United Nations agencies, government authorities and the private sector.

questions.question: The International Day for the Abolition of Slavery is observed in the month of _____?
questions.explanation: The International Day for the Abolition of Slavery, 2 December, marks the date of the adoption, by the General Assembly, of the United Nations Convention for the Suppression of the Traffic in Persons and of the Exploitation of the Prostitution of Others. The focus of this day is on eradicating contemporary forms of slavery, such as trafficking in persons, sexual exploitation, the worst forms of child labour, forced marriage, and the forced recruitment of children for use in armed conflict.

questions.question: 2 November is observed as an international day in relation to which among the following professionals?
questions.explanation: The United Nations General Assembly proclaimed 2 November as the 'International Day to End Impunity for Crimes against Journalists' in General Assembly Resolution. The Resolution urged Member States to implement definite measures countering the present culture of impunity. The date was chosen in commemoration of the assassination of two French journalists in Mali on 2 November 2013.

questions.question: World Development Information Day was established in which year?
questions.explanation: The General Assembly in 1972 established World Development Information Day to draw the attention of the world to development problems and the need to strengthen international cooperation to solve them. It is observed on 24ᵗʰ October.

questions.question: 28ᵗʰ September is celebrated annually to raise awareness about which among the following diseases?
questions.explanation: 28 September is World Rabies Day. It is celebrated annually to raise awareness about rabies prevention and to highlight progress in defeating this horrifying disease.

questions.question: International Day against Nuclear Tests is observed on 29ᵗʰ of which month?
questions.explanation: The 64th session of the United Nations General Assembly declared 29 August the International Day against Nuclear Tests by unanimously adopting a resolution. The resolution calls for increasing awareness and education "about the effects of nuclear weapon test explosions or any other nuclear explosions and the need for their cessation as one of the means of achieving the goal of a nuclear-weapon-free world."

questions.question: United Nations celebrates June 6ᵗʰ as the Day of the which Language?
questions.explanation: June 6, the birthday of the great Russian poet A.S. Pushkin, within the framework of the program of support and development of multilingualism and cultural diversity, the United Nations celebrates the Day of the Russian Language. One of the goals of this program is to maintain the equality of all six official languages ​​of the UN: English, Arabic, Spanish, Chinese, Russian and French.

questions.question: International Day of Families is observed on _____ of May?
questions.explanation: International Day of Families is observed on the 15th of May. The Day highlights the importance of all caregivers in families, be it parents, grandparents or siblings and the importance of parental education for the welfare of children. It focuses on good practices for work-family balance to assist parents in their educational and caregiving roles.

questions.question: Which among the following language is observed on the same day as English Language day?
questions.explanation: English Language Day at the UN is celebrated on 23 April, the date traditionally observed as both the birthday and date of death of William Shakespeare. April 23 also marks the "Day of the Spanish Language" at the United Nations to raise awareness among the staff of the Organization and the world in general about the history, culture and use of Spanish as an official language.

questions.question: 7ᵗʰ April is designated as an international day in relation to which country?
questions.explanation: On 23 December 2003, the United Nations General Assembly adopted resolution designating 7 April, the start date of the 1994 genocide in Rwanda, as the International Day of Reflection on the Genocide in Rwanda.

questions.question: world Wildlife day is observed in the month of _______?
questions.explanation: It is observed on 3ʳᵈ of March. World Wildlife Day is an opportunity to celebrate the many beautiful and varied forms of wild fauna and flora and to raise awareness of the multitude of benefits that conservation provides to people.

questions.question: World Malaria Day is observed on 25ᵗʰ of ______?
questions.explanation: World Malaria Day is an occasion to highlight the need for continued investment and sustained political commitment for malaria prevention and control. It is observed on 25ᵗʰ of April.

questions.question: World Telecommunication and Information Society Day was first jointly observed in the year ________?
questions.explanation: In November 2006, the ITU Plenipotentiary Conference in Antalya, Turkey, decided to celebrate both events on 17 May as World Telecommunication and Information Society Day.

questions.question: International Widows Day is observed on ______ of June?
questions.explanation: It is observed on 23ʳᵈ of June every year. International Widows Day is an opportunity for action towards achieving full rights and recognition for widows - too long invisible, uncounted and ignored.

questions.question: Micro-, Small and Medium-sized Enterprises Day is celebrated in the month of ____?
questions.explanation: The General Assembly, recognizing the need to improve small business access to microfinance and credit, decided to designate 27 June as Micro-, Small and Medium-sized Enterprises Day through its resolution. The goal is to encourage member states to facilitate observance of the day by increasing awareness and actions to support small business.

questions.question: Which among the following day falls in the month of August?
options: World Day against Trafficking in Persons
International Day of the World's Indigenous Peoples
questions.explanation: International Day of the World's Indigenous Peoples is celebrated on 9 August. The General Assembly adopted the United Nations Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples, a major milestone with respect to the cooperation and solidarity between indigenous peoples and Member States.

questions.question: International Day for Universal Access to Information was designated by ______?
questions.explanation: On 17 November 2015, UNESCO adopted a resolution declaring 28 September of every year as International Day for Universal Access to Information.
